claim_boundary: Mesh confidence covers only registered parent/child boundaries and current consumed evidence; child-local green or background progress is not parent proof.

closure_rules.scope: current target revision and declared route only

forbidden_shortcuts.summary: Do not create a SkillGuard-owned alternate execution route.
Do not convert configured booleans, generated JSON, or progress into native pass evidence.
Do not skip required stages, checks, receipts, or blockers.

integration_claim_boundary: SkillGuard gates the target-native FlowGuard route; it does not execute a parallel controller or replace native model/test evidence.

native_check_inventory.summary: Run the tracked hierarchical model-mesh and reattachment tests.

native_route_bindings.source: FlowGuard canonical route registry and target contract source

not_parallel_route_proof.evidence_source: Canonical route registry, contract source, and may_define_* false gates
not_parallel_route_proof.summary: SkillGuard only validates the FlowGuard-owned route and cannot define an alternate successful execution path.

phases.summary: Name the parent claim, child set, model level, split/reattachment trigger, and required scope.
Inventory target split, partition ownership, child contracts, evidence tiers/freshness, receipts, siblings, and closure tokens.
Review coverage/disjointness, current reattachment, parent receipt consumption, affected siblings, leaf boundaries, and closure liveness.
Run the hierarchical mesh model and reject overlap, stale/unconsumed evidence, missing closure, or no-delta loops.
Return parent/child confidence only for current consumed evidence and route unresolved cells/receipts to owners.

quality_floors.summary: Prompt prose, progress, or generated records cannot replace native evidence.
Missing, stale, skipped, scoped, progress-only, or pass-with-gaps evidence cannot support broad closure.

required_evidence.source: FlowGuard native command receipt or direct target artifact
FlowGuard native command receipt or direct target artifact
FlowGuard native command receipt or direct target artifact
FlowGuard native command receipt or direct target artifact
FlowGuard native command receipt or direct target artifact

route_inventory.summary: Govern model hierarchy through target split derivation, disjoint ownership, current child reattachment, receipts, siblings, and closure liveness.

do_not_use_when: The work is an ordinary single model, a test hierarchy, or a code structure split.
routes.summary: Govern model hierarchy through target split derivation, disjoint ownership, current child reattachment, receipts, siblings, and closure liveness.

runtime_lock_policy.applies_when: The flowguard-model-mesh skill is selected or delegated.
runtime_lock_policy.summary: The target-local contract, route, stage order, checks, evidence, and coverage matrix gate closure.

source_requirements.summary: Missing child-output consumers, joins, feedback, progress/bounds, or affected-sibling review blocks broad parent closure.
Parent confidence consumes current child evidence, runtime paths, coverage receipts, and stable handoff contracts.
Target children derive from a named source model and cover parent partitions without unsafe overlap.
Accept a parent and bounded children; derive/verify partitions and current receipts; block overlap, stale/unconsumed child evidence, missing closure/liveness, or incomplete leaf boundaries;.
description: Use when a FlowGuard project has three or more local models, an oversized model, stale child evidence, parent/child partitioning, target split derivation, child reattachment, a.
Route id: model_mesh_maintenance; role: public_owner; native owner: model_mesh_maintenance. This standalone FlowGuard satellite skill owns model hierarchy, not test or code splits.
Use for 3+ models, oversized/incomplete model groups, changed child boundaries, stale child evidence, coverage receipts, affected siblings, or parent whole-flow claims.
The target section 'Use When' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.
Background progress is liveness only; missing closure feedback/bounds or template harvest closure blocks broad mesh confidence.
Edit .skillguard/contract-source.json, then regenerate derived contracts; SkillGuard checks the native mesh contract and cannot reattach children or manufacture receipts.
The target section 'Hard Gates' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.
Parent confidence requires complete partition ownership, legal overlap, current child evidence/receipts, and current parent consumption.
Return evidence, failures, blockers, skipped_checks, residual_risk, claim_boundary, and typed_next_actions, plus a mesh diagram, reattachment, siblings, and receipt status.
Verify the real FlowGuard check engine and AGENTS.md managed record; never create a fake mini-framework.
The target skill description must be represented in the contract: Use when a FlowGuard project has three or more local models, an oversized model, stale child evidence, parent/child partiti.
The target skill entrypoint and hard gates must be represented by the runtime contract.
Declared hard gates and required workflow steps must be represented by checks and closure blockers.
Do not split tests or code, trust child-local green as parent proof, or use for ordinary single-model work; return that work to model-first-function-flow.
The target section 'Do Not Use When' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.
The target section 'Output Requirements' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.
The target section 'FlowGuard Model Mesh' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.
Govern parent/child model ownership, evidence freshness, reattachment, and closure without expanding every child state graph into the parent.
Inventory parent/children, risk boundaries, target split derivation, ownership partitions, evidence tiers, and freshness.
Preserve scoped/stale gaps and project cases/receipts to Model-Test Alignment, TestMesh, and closure owners.
Read references/model_mesh_protocol.md for inventory, target split derivation, partition rules, Child Reattachment Gate, mesh closure, and evidence tiers/freshness.
The target section 'Required Workflow' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.
Review child disjointness, current reattachment, affected siblings, coverage receipts, leaf boundaries, and closure/liveness.
